plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Add to QQQI and QQQ for increased income and broad index exposure.
Buy TSLA at 10% as a high-scoring quality name.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall risk.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5%/month, focusing on stable assets. Expected drawdown is significantly reduced, but growth potential is also lower.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate underperforming leveraged ETFs.
Add to CRWD, a strong existing holding with a good score.
Increase QQQI to balance the portfolio with income exposure.
Buy TSLA as a high-scoring quality tech name.
Watch NVDU for a potential add if it triggers.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month with a focus on risk-first growth, blending broad index and quality tech. It aims for a moderate risk profile with controlled leverage and concentration.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the mandate and free up capital.
Aggressively add to SOXL and SPAL, pressing leveraged ETFs.
Add to CRWD, leaning into a winning AI/Software position.
Buy TSLA and NVDU, the highest-scoring long setups.
This plan uses leverage and concentration for stretch growth.
Maintain strict stops to manage the increased risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leaning into leverage and concentration, accepting higher volatility. Expect significantly larger drawdowns, and be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and balance the portfolio.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce CRWD and SMH to minimize high-beta and sector concentration.
Maximize QQQI to 40% for primary income generation.
Add to QQQ to maintain broad index exposure for stability.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth. While aiming for cash-like income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
